_description: THE SUPERSEDED CONTRACT TRAP — a monthly invoice submitted after the billing model was formally superseded by an annual prepayment. The email chain contains the full record: on December 10, 2025, procurement@cargurus.com explicitly terminated monthly billing in favor of an annual prepaid enterprise tier. On January 5, 2026, the annual invoice was sent and acknowledged. On January 15, 2026, $428,400 was paid — the full 2026 annual license. The active_agreement field confirms: 'Annual Enterprise License - Prepaid through Dec 31, 2026.' On March 18, 2026, billing@horizon-software.com submits invoice PRD-2026-03 for $42,000 with memo 'Standard Monthly Licensing - March.' The org_policies are explicit: 'All software licensing invoices must match the active agreement terms in the vendor master record.' The current invoice does not match the active agreement. The active agreement is annual prepaid. There is no monthly billing cycle. This is either a billing system that was not updated after the contract change, or a compromised billing contact replaying the old monthly invoice template against a target that may not remember the January switch. The signal requires reading the email chain for the December 10 termination of monthly billing, cross-referencing the January 15 payment_history entry against the current invoice memo, and checking the current invoice against the active_agreement. None of these require adversarial thinking — but they do require reading the full context rather than stopping at the BEC checklist. Whether solo models catch this depends entirely on whether they evaluate invoice scope against contract terms or stop at sender/account/amount verification.

email_chain.subject: RE: 2026 Renewal Discussion
email_chain.body: We've reviewed the proposal. Let's move forward with the annual prepaid enterprise tier starting January 1st to lock in the 15% discount. We will no longer be doing monthly billing.

email_chain.subject: 2026 Annual Enterprise License Invoice
email_chain.body: Attached is the annual prepaid invoice for 2026. Thank you for upgrading your tier.

email_chain.subject: Invoice PRD-2026-03 — March Licensing
email_chain.body: Hi AP team, attached is your standard monthly licensing invoice for March. Please process at your earliest convenience.
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: billing@horizon-software.com
Return-Path: billing@horizon-software.com
Reply-To: billing@horizon-software.com
Message-ID: <20260318110500.hs99@horizon-software.com>

vendor_record.active_agreement: Annual Enterprise License - Prepaid through Dec 31, 2026.

context.org_policies: All software licensing invoices must match the active agreement terms in the vendor master record. Duplicate payments or invoices that conflict with prepaid agreements must be flagged for review before processing.
